This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   The user is building "Inventorize" — a multi-tenant SaaS inventory management platform — using the Spec-Driven Platform V26 framework. The session covered: (1) resuming the session with project context, (2) running Phase 2 discovery interview to lock all technical decisions, (3) running Phase 2.7 spec stress-test which found 5 gaps in PRODUCT.md and resolved all of them with user input, and (4) starting Phase 3 spec file generation, which is currently mid-execution. The user's intent is to complete Phase 3 so Cline can proceed to Phase 4 (monorepo scaffold).

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - Spec-Driven Platform V26 — 30-rule governance framework with 8-phase build process
   - Multi-tenant SaaS with subdirectory routing (/[tenant-slug]/...)
   - 6 security layers: L1 tRPC tenant scoping, L2 Prisma middleware, L3 RBAC, L4 PgBouncer, L5 AuditLog, L6 Prisma query guardrails — ALL ACTIVE (multi-tenant mode)
   - Auth.js v5 with Credentials provider, sessions in PostgreSQL, VerificationToken for first-login setup links
   - Stack: Next.js (App Router) + tRPC + Prisma + Valkey + BullMQ + MinIO + shadcn/ui + Tailwind CSS
   - Docker Hub: DISABLED (docker.publish: false)
   - Phase 2.7 spec stress-test: 4-category check (completeness, consistency, ambiguity, security)
   - Port generation: random base 44874 in 40000–49999 range (Rule 22)
   - All credentials generated via openssl (Rule: never hallucinate credentials)
   - URL-encoding required for passwords containing `/` and `+` in DATABASE_URL

3. Files and Code Sections:

   - `docs/PRODUCT.md` (MODIFIED — 7 additive edits from Phase 2.7 gap fixes):
     - Flow 1 rewritten: slug auto-generated from name, editable field, real-time uniqueness check, URL preview shown; welcome email now uses one-time setup token (24h expiry, /auth/setup?token=[token])
     - Flow 2 updated: impersonation enforcement added — server-side tRPC FORBIDDEN on mutations when isImpersonating=true, client-side UI disabled, "View Only" banner
     - Flow 12 ADDED: tenant dashboard user flow — KPI cards, period selector, inventory value hidden from warehouse_staff, low stock banner
     - Platform Administration module: Tenant create form bullet updated to note editable slug + URL preview
     - Key Business Rules: printableSlipNumber rule added: `'SO-' + zero-padded 5-digit sequential per tenant`
     - Access Control/Tenant isolation: impersonation mutation blocking rule added
     - Tech Stack Preferences: VerificationToken table added

   - `inputs.yml` (CREATED — Phase 3 output):
     ```yaml
     app:
       name: Inventorize
       display_name: "Inventorize by Powerbyte"
       slug: inventorize
     domains:
       prod: inventorize.powerbyte.app
       staging: inventorize-staging.powerbyte.app
     tenancy:
       mode: multi
       routing: subdirectory
       url_pattern: "/[tenant-slug]"
       slug_generation: auto_from_name
       slug_collision: editable_field_with_inline_error
     auth:
       provider: authjs_v5
       strategy: credentials
       session_storage: postgresql
       session_fields: [userId, tenantId, roles, name, email, isImpersonating]
       first_login: one_time_setup_link
       setup_token_expiry_hours: 24
       login_error_message: "Invalid credentials."
     jobs:
       enabled: true
       provider: bullmq
       queues:
         - name: low-stock-check
           cron: "0 0 * * *"
         - name: email-notifications
           cron: null
     storage:
       enabled: true
       provider: minio
       allowed_mime_types: [application/pdf, image/jpeg, image/png]
       max_file_size_mb: 10
     docker:
       publish: false
     security:
       rate_limits:
         public_per_min: 60
         authenticated_per_min: 300
         upload_per_min: 10
       cors:
         dev: "localhost:*"
         staging: "https://inventorize-staging.powerbyte.app"
         prod: "https://inventorize.powerbyte.app"
     git:
       default_branch: main
       branch_pattern: feat/{slug}
       commit_style: conventional
       squash_merge: true
       use_worktrees: true
     models:
       planning: claude-code
       execution: minimax-m2.5
       governance: gemini-2.5-flash-lite
     ports:
       dev:
         base: 44874
         db: 44874
         pgbouncer: 44875
         redis: 44876
         minio: 44877
         minio_console: 44878
         mailhog_smtp: 44879
         mailhog_ui: 44880
         pgadmin: 44881
         app: 44884
         worker: 44885
         studio: 44894
     ```

   - `inputs.schema.json` (CREATED — JSON Schema validation for inputs.yml):
     - Full JSON Schema with required fields, enum constraints, type validation
     - Validates: app, domains, tenancy, auth, apps, tech_stack, jobs, storage, mobile, docker, security, git, models, ports
     - ports.dev.base constrained: minimum 40000, maximum 49999

   - `CREDENTIALS.md` (EXISTS from Bootstrap, NOT YET UPDATED with Phase 3 values)

4. Errors and Fixes:
   - Phase 2.7 found 5 PRODUCT.md gaps before Phase 3 could proceed:
     - GAP 1 (tenant dashboard missing flow): Fixed by adding Flow 12
     - GAP 2 (impersonation enforcement undefined): Fixed by specifying server-side tRPC FORBIDDEN + client UI disabled
     - GAP 3 (welcome email login mechanism undefined): User chose Option A — one-time password reset link (24h, single-use token)
     - GAP 4 (slug collision handling undefined): User chose Option B — editable field pre-filled + inline error
     - GAP 5 (printableSlipNumber format undefined): Fixed with 'SO-' + zero-padded 5-digit sequential per tenant
   - URL-encoding issue: Passwords generated with openssl base64 contain `/` and `+` which are not URL-safe. Resolved by computing percent-encoded variants for use in DATABASE_URL strings:
     - DB_PASS_DEV `/` → `%2F`: `2fCw%2FBpl0X340zr44GXtoS`
     - DB_PASS_STG `/` → `%2F`: `5cIThIDpP%2FWGTIGoGycwuM`
     - DB_PASS_PRD `+` → `%2B`: `VPCZ00IaW7GZdx5%2B1bQF54`
     - PGB_PASS_STG `+` → `%2B`: `ydnmqWgq%2BAfMpa5DAFbjy4`

5. Problem Solving:
   - Phase 2.7 stress-test successfully caught 5 gaps before code generation began, preventing Cline from halting or guessing during Phase 4
   - All Phase 2 decisions locked in inputs.yml to prevent re-asking
   - Real openssl credentials generated (not hallucinated) per Rule compliance
   - URL-safe credential encoding computed to prevent DATABASE_URL parse failures with Prisma

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="Bash"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Bash**</summary>
Generate all Phase 3 credentials via openssl

```bash

# Generate all credentials for Phase 3
# DB passwords (22-char)
DB_PASS_DEV=$(openssl rand -base64 32 | tr -d '\n' | head -c 22)
DB_PASS_STG=$(openssl rand -base64 32 | tr -d '\n' | head -c 22)
DB_PASS_PRD=$(openssl rand -base64 32 | tr -d '\n' | head -c 22)

# DB usernames (hex suffix)
DB_USER_SUFFIX_DEV=$(openssl rand -hex 11)
DB_USER_SUFFIX_STG=$(openssl rand -hex 11)
DB_USER_SUFFIX_PRD=$(openssl rand -hex 11)

# PgBouncer passwords
PGB_PASS_DEV=$(openssl rand -base64 32 | tr -d '\n' | head -c 22)
PGB_PASS_STG=$(openssl rand -base64 32 | tr -d '\n' | head -c 22)
PGB_PASS_PRD=$(openssl rand -base64 32 | tr -d '\n' | head -c 22)

# Valkey/Redis passwords
RDS_PASS_DEV=$(openssl rand -base64 32 | tr -d '\n' | head -c 22)
RDS_PASS_STG=$(openssl rand -base64 32 | tr -d '\n' | head -c 22)
RDS_PASS_PRD=$(openssl rand -base64 32 | tr -d '\n' | head -c 22)

# MinIO keys
MINIO_KEY_SUFFIX_DEV=$(openssl rand -hex 11)
MINIO_KEY_SUFFIX_STG=$(openssl rand -hex 11)
MINIO_KEY_SUFFIX_PRD=$(openssl rand -hex 11)
MINIO_SECRET_DEV=$(openssl rand -base64 32 | tr -d '\n' | head -c 48)
MINIO_SECRET_STG=$(openssl rand -base64 32 | tr -d '\n' | head -c 48)
MINIO_SECRET_PRD=$(openssl rand -base64 32 | tr -d '\n' | head -c 48)

# pgAdmin passwords
PGA_PASS_DEV=$(openssl rand -base64 32 | tr -d '\n' | head -c 22)
PGA_PASS_STG=$(openssl rand -base64 32 | tr -d '\n' | head -c 22)
PGA_PASS_PRD=$(openssl rand -base64 32 | tr -d '\n' | head -c 22)

# pgAdmin emails
PGA_EMAIL_SUFFIX_DEV=$(openssl rand -hex 4)
PGA_EMAIL_SUFFIX_STG=$(openssl rand -hex 4)
PGA_EMAIL_SUFFIX_PRD=$(openssl rand -hex 4)

# Auth secrets (48-char)
AUTH_SECRET_DEV=$(openssl rand -base64 64 | tr -d '\n' | head -c 48)
AUTH_SECRET_STG=$(openssl rand -base64 64 | tr -d '\n' | head -c 48)
AUTH_SECRET_PRD=$(openssl rand -base64 64 | tr -d '\n' | head -c 48)

# Webmaster password
WEBMASTER_PASS=$(openssl rand -base64 32 | tr -d '\n' | head -c 22)

# URL-encode passwords (replace / with %2F, + with %2B)
url_encode() {
  echo "$1" | sed 's|/|%2F|g' | sed 's|+|%2B|g'
}

DB_PASS_DEV_ENC=$(url_encode "$DB_PASS_DEV")
DB_PASS_STG_ENC=$(url_encode "$DB_PASS_STG")
DB_PASS_PRD_ENC=$(url_encode "$DB_PASS_PRD")
PGB_PASS_DEV_ENC=$(url_encode "$PGB_PASS_DEV")
PGB_PASS_STG_ENC=$(url_encode "$PGB_PASS_STG")
PGB_PASS_PRD_ENC=$(url_encode "$PGB_PASS_PRD")

# Print all for verification
echo "=== DB ==="
echo "DB_USER_DEV=inventorize_${DB_USER_SUFFIX_DEV}"
echo "DB_PASS_DEV=${DB_PASS_DEV}"
echo "DB_PASS_DEV_ENC=${DB_PASS_DEV_ENC}"
echo "DB_USER_STG=inventorize_${DB_USER_SUFFIX_STG}"
echo "DB_PASS_STG=${DB_PASS_STG}"
echo "DB_PASS_STG_ENC=${DB_PASS_STG_ENC}"
echo "DB_USER_PRD=inventorize_${DB_USER_SUFFIX_PRD}"
echo "DB_PASS_PRD=${DB_PASS_PRD}"
echo "DB_PASS_PRD_ENC=${DB_PASS_PRD_ENC}"
echo "=== PGBOUNCER ==="
echo "PGB_PASS_DEV=${PGB_PASS_DEV}"
echo "PGB_PASS_DEV_ENC=${PGB_PASS_DEV_ENC}"
echo "PGB_PASS_STG=${PGB_PASS_STG}"
echo "PGB_PASS_STG_ENC=${PGB_PASS_STG_ENC}"
echo "PGB_PASS_PRD=${PGB_PASS_PRD}"
echo "PGB_PASS_PRD_ENC=${PGB_PASS_PRD_ENC}"
echo "=== VALKEY ==="
echo "RDS_PASS_DEV=${RDS_PASS_DEV}"
echo "RDS_PASS_STG=${RDS_PASS_STG}"
echo "RDS_PASS_PRD=${RDS_PASS_PRD}"
echo "=== MINIO ==="
echo "MINIO_KEY_DEV=inventorize-${MINIO_KEY_SUFFIX_DEV}"
echo "MINIO_SECRET_DEV=${MINIO_SECRET_DEV}"
echo "MINIO_KEY_STG=inventorize-${MINIO_KEY_SUFFIX_STG}"
echo "MINIO_SECRET_STG=${MINIO_SECRET_STG}"
echo "MINIO_KEY_PRD=inventorize-${MINIO_KEY_SUFFIX_PRD}"
echo "MINIO_SECRET_PRD=${MINIO_SECRET_PRD}"
echo "=== PGADMIN ==="
echo "PGA_EMAIL_DEV=inventorize-${PGA_EMAIL_SUFFIX_DEV}@admin.local"
echo "PGA_PASS_DEV=${PGA_PASS_DEV}"
echo "PGA_EMAIL_STG=inventorize-${PGA_EMAIL_SUFFIX_STG}@admin.local"
echo "PGA_PASS_STG=${PGA_PASS_STG}"
echo "PGA_EMAIL_PRD=inventorize-${PGA_EMAIL_SUFFIX_PRD}@admin.local"
echo "PGA_PASS_PRD=${PGA_PASS_PRD}"
echo "=== AUTH ==="
echo "AUTH_SECRET_DEV=${AUTH_SECRET_DEV}"
echo "AUTH_SECRET_STG=${AUTH_SECRET_STG}"
echo "AUTH_SECRET_PRD=${AUTH_SECRET_PRD}"
echo "=== WEBMASTER ==="
echo "WEBMASTER_PASS=${WEBMASTER_PASS}"

```

```prod
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════
# PRODUCTION environment — generated by Phase 3
# Mono-server: DB + cache + storage all on same host
# To move any service to external: update HOST var
# and remove that service from docker-compose.*.yml
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════
COMPOSE_PROJECT_NAME=inventorize_prod
APP_ENV=production
APP_PORT=3000

# DOCKER HUB IMAGE (docker.publish: false — no image pipeline)
# APP_IMAGE_TAG=latest

# DATABASE
DB_HOST=localhost
DB_PORT=5432
DB_NAME=inventorize_prod
DB_USER=inventorize_127dcea6b108d76d78b06f
DB_PASSWORD=Qg657GzRvI3fE4d61mM1oB
DATABASE_URL=postgresql://inventorize_127dcea6b108d76d78b06f:Qg657GzRvI3fE4d61mM1oB@localhost:5432/inventorize_prod?schema=public

PGBOUNCER_PORT=6432
PGBOUNCER_AUTH_PASSWORD=7TES85HBQ7Y1k+WxLIzgLK
PGBOUNCER_DATABASE_URL=postgresql://inventorize_127dcea6b108d76d78b06f:7TES85HBQ7Y1k%2BWxLIzgLK@localhost:6432/inventorize_prod

# CACHE (Valkey)
REDIS_HOST=localhost
REDIS_PORT=6379
REDIS_PASSWORD=YEvywXD5m2xx8cXccF/YRU
REDIS_URL=redis://:YEvywXD5m2xx8cXccF%2FYRU@localhost:6379

# FILE STORAGE (MinIO — S3-compatible)

STORAGE_CONSOLE_PORT=9001

STORAGE_ACCESS_KEY=inventorize-6a0470873caa81dbdd7d17
```

```example
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Inventorize by Powerbyte — Environment Variables Template
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# HOW TO USE:
# 1. Copy this file: cp .env.example .env.dev
# 2. Fill in real values (or run Phase 3 to auto-generate them)
# 3. NEVER commit .env.dev, .env.staging, or .env.prod to git
# 4. NEVER commit CREDENTIALS.md to git (it is gitignored)
#
# FIRST ADMIN ACCOUNT (created by pnpm db:seed):
#   Username: webmaster
#   Password: see CREDENTIALS.md (gitignored — generated by Phase 3)
#   URL: http://localhost:[APP_PORT]/login
#
# All service passwords in CREDENTIALS.md are AI-generated strong passwords.
# Anyone cloning this repo must generate their own via Phase 3.
# ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

COMPOSE_PROJECT_NAME=inventorize_dev
APP_ENV=development|staging|production
APP_PORT=44884

# DATABASE
DB_HOST=localhost
DB_PORT=44874
DB_NAME=inventorize_dev
DB_USER=inventorize_your-hex-suffix-here
DB_PASSWORD=your-db-password-here
DATABASE_URL=postgresql://${DB_USER}:${DB_PASSWORD}@${DB_HOST}:${DB_PORT}/${DB_NAME}?schema=public

# PGBOUNCER
PGBOUNCER_PORT=44875
PGBOUNCER_AUTH_PASSWORD=your-pgbouncer-password-here
PGBOUNCER_DATABASE_URL=postgresql://${DB_USER}:${PGBOUNCER_AUTH_PASSWORD}@localhost:${PGBOUNCER_PORT}/${DB_NAME}

# CACHE (Valkey)
REDIS_HOST=localhost
REDIS_PORT=44876
REDIS_PASSWORD=your-redis-password-here
REDIS_URL=redis://:${REDIS_PASSWORD}@${REDIS_HOST}:${REDIS_PORT}

# FILE STORAGE (MinIO — S3-compatible)
STORAGE_ENDPOINT=http://localhost:44877
STORAGE_CONSOLE_PORT=44878
STORAGE_BUCKET=inventorize-dev
STORAGE_ACCESS_KEY=inventorize-your-access-key-hex-here
STORAGE_SECRET_KEY=your-storage-secret-key-here
STORAGE_REGION=us-east-1

# AUTH
AUTH_SECRET=your-48-char-auth-secret-here
NEXTAUTH_URL=http://localhost:44884

# EMAIL (MailHog in dev — no credentials needed)
SMTP_HOST=localhost
SMTP_PORT=44879
SMTP_UI_PORT=44880
SMTP_FROM=dev@localhost
# For staging/prod:
# SMTP_HOST=smtp.yourdomain.com
# SMTP_PORT=587
# SMTP_USER=noreply@yourdomain.com
# SMTP_PASSWORD=your-smtp-password-here

# PGADMIN
PGADMIN_PORT=44881
PGADMIN_EMAIL=your-pgadmin-email-here
PGADMIN_PASSWORD=your-pgadmin-password-here

# WORKER
WORKER_PORT=44885

# PRISMA STUDIO
STUDIO_PORT=44894

```

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-04-03 05:25:59Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./docs/DECISIONS_LOG.md`

```text
1	# Decisions Log — Spec-Driven Platform V26
2	# Format: ## YYYY-MM-DD — [Decision title]
3	# Types: 🟤 decision | ⚖️ trade-off
4	# Never re-ask anything listed here. These are locked decisions.
5	# ---
6	
7	## Dev Environment Mode
8	- Type:      🟤 decision
9	- Date:      2026-04-02
10	- Decision:  MODE A — WSL2 native (the only supported mode as of V25)
11	- Rationale: Devcontainer adds 4 virtualisation layers on WSL2 + Docker Desktop causing
12	             permission errors, shell server crashes, and socket failures. WSL2 native eliminates all of this.
13	             Docker Desktop provides the Docker socket to WSL2 natively. No DinD needed.
14	- Locked:    yes — do not re-ask or scaffold devcontainer files.
15	
16	---
17	
18	## Git Branching Strategy
19	- Type:      🟤 decision
20	- Date:      2026-04-02
21	- Decision:  feat/{slug}, scaffold/part-{N}, squash-merge
22	- Rationale: Phase 4 uses part-by-part execution (Rule 24). Each Part gets its own branch.
23	             Feature branches use feat/{slug}. Never commit directly to main.
24	- Locked:    yes — follows Rule 23
25	
26	---
27	
28	## Model Routing
29	- Type:      🟤 decision
30	- Date:      2026-04-02
31	- Decision:  Planning/execution/governance model assignments
32	- Rationale: Planning: claude-code (Phase 2) or minimax-m2.5 (Cline)
33	             Execution: minimax-m2.5 (default — free tier, Cline via OpenRouter)
34	             Governance: gemini-2.5-flash-lite (cheapest, non-critical writes)
35	- Locked:    yes — follows Rule 24
36
```
</details></tool-use>
